activities_projects.title: Convection Current and Plate Tectonics Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Mathematically correlate heat flow in the mantle to the speed of lithospheric plates
steps: Heat the viscous liquid and measure the temperature gradient from bottom to top
Drop sawdust to track the 'convection cells' and measure the speed of the surface current
Research the actual drift speed of the Indian Plate (approx. 5 cm/year)
Explain the 'Mantle Drag' mechanism and how it causes seafloor spreading
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the thermodynamic drivers of endogenic forces and plate motion
activities_projects.evaluation: Data-driven explanation of the relationship between heat and velocity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Seismic Wave Propagation and Epicenter Triangulation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the difference between P-waves and S-waves in seismic monitoring
steps: Demonstrate longitudinal (P) and transverse (S) waves using the spring
Given arrival times of waves at three different stations, use the formula $d = v 	imes t$ to find distances
Use the compass to draw three circles on a map; the intersection is the 'Epicenter'
Explain why the 'Shadow Zone' exists based on the liquid state of the outer core
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates how seismic data is used to pinpoint the focus of an earthquake
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the triangulation and wave behavior analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Quantitative Meander Migration Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the rate of lateral erosion in a river system
materials: Historical maps of a local river (10-year gap)
steps: Overlay a current river map with a map from 10 years ago
Measure the distance the 'Meander Neck' has moved closer (lateral erosion)
Predict the 'Year of Cut-off' when the meander will turn into an Ox-bow lake
Discuss the role of centrifugal force in eroding the outer bank
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Calculates the rate of landscape evolution through fluvial processes
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision in measurement and logical prediction of the ox-bow formation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Glacial Abrasion and Plucking Mechanics
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the physical forces behind 'U-shaped' valley formation
steps: Freeze sand into the bottom of an ice block to simulate 'plucking' and 'abrasion' tools
Slide the weighted ice block over the clay surface multiple times
Observe the deep striations (scratches) and the change in the valley profile from V to U
Contrast this with the V-shaped valley formed by river vertical erosion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between the erosional signatures of water and ice
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical description of striations and valley morphology (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Physics of Mushroom Rock Stability
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the 'Bernoulli Effect' in wind erosion and center of gravity
materials: Scale model of a mushroom rock (sand/clay)
steps: Simulate a sandstorm using the fan and dry sand against a rock pillar
Explain why sand concentration is highest at 0.5m to 1m above the ground (saltation)
Calculate the 'Toppling Point'—at what stage of erosion will the top-heavy rock collapse?
Research 'Ventifacts' and their role in identifying paleo-wind directions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the aerodynamic and gravitational factors in desert landforms
activities_projects.evaluation: Scientific reasoning for the rock's unique shape and stability limit (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Deltaic Evolution and Subsidence Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the formation of distributaries and bird's foot deltas
steps: Let a river flow into a 'sea' (water basin) at the end of the tray
Observe the 'Bifurcation'—the splitting into distributaries as the mouth gets choked
Vary the 'sea level' and observe the change in delta shape
Research the impact of dams on the 'Silt Budget' and delta shrinkage
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects sediment load to the growth and recession of coastal landforms
activities_projects.evaluation: Analysis of the 'Silt Budget' and its impact on delta health (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Earthquake Magnitude vs. Intensity Research
activities_projects.objective: Distinguish between the Richter Scale and the Mercalli Scale
materials: Case studies of Bhuj (2001) and Nepal (2015) earthquakes
steps: Plot the energy release difference between Magnitude 6 and 7 (approx. 32 times)
Map the 'Isoseismal lines' (lines of equal damage intensity) for a major quake
Explain why an earthquake of the same magnitude can have different intensities in different regions
Discuss the role of soil type (liquefaction) in increasing destruction
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically analyzes the factors that determine earthquake damage beyond energy release
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity in the distinction between log-scale magnitude and observation-scale intensity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Coastal Recession and 'Stack' Lifecycle
activities_projects.objective: Predict the collapse of coastal features using erosion rates
materials: Photographs of 'The Twelve Apostles' (Australia) over 50 years
steps: Measure the thinning of a 'Sea Arch' pillar over several decades of photos
Calculate the average rate of erosion (cm/year)
Estimate when the arch will become a stack and when the stack will disappear
Discuss how global sea-level rise accelerates this exogenic process
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies mathematical modeling to predict the lifespan of coastal landforms
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of erosion rate calculations and future projections (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Endogenic-Exogenic Feedback Loops
activities_projects.objective: Study how mountain building (endogenic) affects climate and erosion (exogenic)
steps: Trace how the rise of the Himalayas (Plate Tectonics) created the Monsoon system
Analyze how high rainfall then increases the rate of river erosion
Discuss the concept of 'Isostatic Rebound'—as mountains erode, they become lighter and rise higher
Diagram this 'Circular Relationship' between internal and external forces
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes the complex interaction between Earth's internal energy and external climate
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of the feedback loop diagram and logical connections (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Seismic Resistant Architecture Challenge
activities_projects.objective: Design a structure that survives a simulated 'Magnitude 8' vibration
steps: Design a building with a 'Base Isolation' or 'Cross-bracing' system
Test the building on the shake table at increasing frequencies
Identify the 'Resonant Frequency' where the building sways most
Write a report on how 'Triangulation' and 'Symmetry' help in seismic safety
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies engineering principles to mitigate the impact of sudden endogenic movements
activities_projects.evaluation: Structural integrity during the shake test and quality of the engineering report (10 marks)
